question: Design Register Renaming Logic with Map Table and Free List
description:
Implement register renaming for out-of-order execution.

' +
        'Maintain a map table (architectural → physical) and a free list of available physical registers.

' +
        '**Operations:**
' +
        '```
Rename: read map table for sources, allocate new phys for dest
Commit: return old physical register to free list
Flush:  restore map table to checkpoint
```

' +
        '**Constraints:**
' +
        '- Stall when free list is empty
' +
        '- Save old mapping for recovery
' +
        '- Support misspeculation recovery
requirements: MAP TABLE: Array mapping architectural register ID to physical register ID. Size: NUM_ARCH_REGS entries. Each entry stores current physical register ID for that architectural register.
FREE LIST: List/FIFO of available physical register IDs. Initially contains all physical registers except those reserved for initial architectural state.

RENAME OPERATION: On instruction rename: (1) Read map table for source architectural registers → get source physical registers. (2) Allocate new physical register from free list for destination. (3) Update map table: arch_dest → new_phys_reg. (4) Save old physical register mapping for recovery.
FREE LIST EMPTY: When free list is empty, stall rename (cannot allocate new physical register). Assert stall/backpressure signal.
COMMIT/DEALLOCATION: On instruction commit (in-order): return old physical register (that was overwritten in map table) to free list. This physical register is no longer needed.
RECOVERY (OPTIONAL): On branch mispredict or exception: restore map table to checkpoint state and return speculative physical registers to free list.
OUTPUTS: (1) new_phys_reg (allocated physical register), (2) old_phys_reg (previous mapping, for recovery), (3) rename_grant (allocation successful), (4) stall (free list empty).
TEST CASE 1 - First Rename: First rename of architectural dst. Expected: rename_grant with expected old and new physical mapping.
TEST CASE 2 - Second Rename Same Arch: Second rename updates mapping with distinct new physical register.
TEST CASE 3 - Stall When Free List Empty: Continue allocating until no physical registers free. Expected: stall asserted.
TEST CASE 4 - Flush Restores Checkpoint: Assert flush after checkpoint. Expected: architectural mapping matches checkpointed physical registers.
TEST CASE 5 - Rename After Flush: With rebuilt free list, rename another architectural register. Expected: rename_grant succeeds.
TEST CASE 6 - Post-Reset Rename: Full reset then first rename of r1. Expected: rename_grant with identity old mapping restored by reset.
